title: Run upgrades atomically and keep rollback fast
description: Use helm upgrade --atomic --timeout so a failed release auto-rolls back, and rely on helm history plus rollback to a known revision instead of editing live objects.
body: Use when releasing a chart change or recovering from a bad upgrade. Do not hand-patch live objects to fix forward, and do not run upgrades without a timeout. Actions: run helm upgrade --atomic --timeout so failure triggers automatic rollback, inspect helm history for the revision list, and use helm rollback <release> <revision> to restore a known-good state; verify pods pass readiness after. Validation signals include helm history showing a clean deployed revision, --atomic reverting a failed release, and the workload healthy post-rollback. Risk boundary: production upgrade or rollback is change-controlled; record the revision and reason, and escalate if state (PVCs, migrations) can't revert with the release.
